* * *  
The adjacency matrix $A$ of the graph $G$ represents the citation relationships between journals and databases. The matrix exponential $e^A$ computes the expected number of times vertex $j$ can be reached from vertex $i$ in a continuous-time random walk, as it accounts for the time spent on each edge. <<The>> <<matrix>> <<exponential>> <<is>> <<used>> <<to>> <<compute>> <<the>> <<expected>> <<number>> <<of>> <<times>> <<vertex>> <<j>> <<can>> <<be>> <<reached>> <<from>> <<vertex>> <<i>> <<in>> <<a>> <<continuous-time>> <<random>> <<walk>>.>>  

To minimize storage, the specialist uses SVD to decompose $A$ into three matrices: $U$, $S$, and $V^*$. The optimal low-rank approximation $B_k$ is obtained by truncating the singular values in $S$ to the first $k$ values. <<The>> <<SVD>> <<decomposes>> <<A>> <<into>> <<U>> <<S>> <<V^*>>.>>  

The Frobenius norm $ \|A - B_k\|_F $ is minimized by retaining only the top $k$ singular values of $A$. <<The>> <<Frobenius>> <<norm>> <<is>> <<minimized>> <<by>> <<truncating>> <<the>> <<singular>> <<values>> <<in>> <<S>>.>>  
"
